perturbation.original_text: S2K shall not act as an underwriter in connection with S2K's wholesale activities relating to shares of the Funds where S2K receives all or substantially all of the sales load, as set forth in each Fund's then-current prospectus ("Prospectus"). S2K will market the Funds to Intermediaries that: (a) are registered as "broker-dealers" with the SEC, FINRA, and any other applicable jurisdiction in which they operate and are required to be so registered by law; Source: CC REAL ESTATE INCOME FUND-ADV, POS 8C, 12/5/2018 (b) will enter into a Selling Agreement agreed to by Distributor and such "broker-dealers," or in such other form of Intermediary agreement (which shall include, without limitation, broker/dealer Selling Agreements, platform agreements and wirehouse agreements) as required by an Intermediary with the Distributor to sell shares of the Funds to investors (copies of which shall be made available to S2K); and (c) will sell shares of the Funds through representatives in accordance with the then-current applicable Prospectus and in accordance with the provisions of the Selling Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: S2K shall not act as an underwriter in connection with S2K's wholesale activities relating to shares of the Funds where S2K receives all or substantially all of the sales load, as set forth in each Fund's then-current prospectus ("Prospectus"). S2K will market the Funds to Intermediaries that: (a) are registered as "investment firms" with the SEC, FINRA, and any other applicable jurisdiction in which they operate and are required to be so registered by law; Source: CC REAL ESTATE INCOME FUND-ADV, POS 8C, 12/5/2018 (b) will enter into a Selling Agreement agreed to by Distributor and such "broker-dealers," or in such other form of Intermediary agreement (which shall include, without limitation, broker/dealer Selling Agreements, platform agreements and wirehouse agreements) as required by an Intermediary with the Distributor to sell shares of the Funds to investors (copies of which shall be made available to S2K); and (c) will sell shares of the Funds through representatives in accordance with the then-current applicable Prospectus and in accordance with the provisions of the Selling Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'broker-dealers' is replaced with 'investment firms'. This creates uncertainty as 'investment firms' is a broader term that may include entities not registered as broker-dealers, leading to confusion about who S2K can market the Funds to and if those investment firms are regulated or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: S2K agrees, subject to the provisions of this Agreement, through its Authorized S2K Representatives, to use its reasonable best efforts to market the Funds to the Intermediaries, and to identify, refer and/or introduce Intermediaries to the Funds. In connection therewith, S2K may (i) engage in seminars, conferences and media interviews for financial intermediaries; (ii) distribute sales literature and other communications (including electronic media) regarding the Funds, subject to review and approval of such material by the Distributor; and (iii) perform other services reasonably contemplated in writing by S2K and the Distributor. S2K shall not act as an underwriter in connection with S2K's wholesale activities relating to shares of the Funds where S2K receives all or substantially all of the sales load, as set forth in each Fund's then-current prospectus ("Prospectus"). S2K will market the Funds to Intermediaries that: (a) are registered as "investment firms" with the SEC, FINRA, and any other applicable jurisdiction in which they operate and are required to be so registered by law; Source: CC REAL ESTATE INCOME FUND-ADV, POS 8C, 12/5/2018 (b) will enter into a Selling Agreement agreed to by Distributor and such "broker-dealers," or in such other form of Intermediary agreement (which shall include, without limitation, broker/dealer Selling Agreements, platform agreements and wirehouse agreements) as required by an Intermediary with the Distributor to sell shares of the Funds to investors (copies of which shall be made available to S2K); and (c) will sell shares of the Funds through representatives in accordance with the then-current applicable Prospectus and in accordance with the provisions of the Selling Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'broker-dealers' with 'investment firms' in the section describing S2K's marketing activities. While the original text specifies marketing to 'broker-dealers,' the altered text broadens this to 'investment firms,' creating a contradiction because it's unclear whether all investment firms are registered as broker-dealers as defined in the original text of section (b) 'Selling Agreement agreed to by Distributor and such "broker-dealers,"'. This change introduces uncertainty about the specific regulatory requirements for these entities, leading to a contradiction as to which type of firms S2K is permitted to market to based on the definition of Intermediaries in section (b) agreed upon by the distributor. Therefore, it creates a meaningful in-text contradiction within the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: S2K shall devote sufficient staff and expenditures to the performance of its services as shall be consistent with industry standards for the marketing of shares of the Fund. S2K shall perform these services in a professional and competent manner and shall provide such office space and equipment, telephone facilities and personnel as it determines may be reasonably necessary or beneficial in order to provide such services at no cost to the Distributor.
perturbation.changed_text: S2K shall devote sufficient staff and expenditures to the performance of its services as shall be consistent with company guidelines for the marketing of shares of the Fund. S2K shall perform these services in a satisfactory manner and shall provide such office space and equipment, telephone facilities and personnel as it determines may be reasonably necessary or beneficial in order to provide such services at no cost to the Distributor.
perturbation.explanation: Replaced "industry standards" with "company guidelines" and "professional and competent manner" with "satisfactory manner." This introduces ambiguity as 'company guidelines' are not defined and could deviate from established industry standards. 'Satisfactory manner' is also subjective, leading to potential disputes over performance assessment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: S2K shall devote sufficient staff and expenditures to the performance of its services as shall be consistent with company guidelines for the marketing of shares of the Fund. S2K shall perform these services in a satisfactory manner and shall provide such office space and equipment, telephone facilities and personnel as it determines may be reasonably necessary or beneficial in order to provide such services at no cost to the Distributor.
perturbation.justification: The Changed Text and the Contradicted Text are identical. The explanation argues that the changes from the Original Text to the Changed Text (specifically, 'industry standards' to 'company guidelines' and 'professional and competent manner' to 'satisfactory manner') introduces ambiguity. However, while this might create potential for future disputes, it doesn't create a direct, irresolvable contradiction within the provided text itself. Both phrases are subjective, but it is not strong enough to trigger a YES.

perturbation.original_text: Each party to this Agreement shall obtain the other party's prior written consent before using any marketing or sales literature related to the consenting party, and shall not use the other party's names in any marketing or advertising materials without prior written consent from the consenting party.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party to this Agreement shall obtain the other party's prior written consent before using any promotional materials related to the consenting party, and shall not use the other party's names in any advertisements without prior agreement from the consenting party.
perturbation.explanation: The terms "marketing or sales literature" are changed to "promotional materials", and "marketing or advertising materials" changed to "advertisements" and "prior written consent" to "prior agreement". These changes introduce inconsistency by not defining the scope of "promotional materials" or "advertisements", or what constitutes "prior agreement" (written vs. verbal), potentially leading to disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party to this Agreement shall obtain the other party's prior written consent before using any promotional materials related to the consenting party, and shall not use the other party's names in any advertisements without prior agreement from the consenting party.
perturbation.justification: Although the terms are altered, the core obligation of obtaining consent before using the other party's promotional or advertising materials remains consistent. The changes don't create a direct, clear conflict in obligations or enforcement. The 'prior agreement' language introduces some ambiguity, but it does not create an explicit contradiction as written consent could be construed to be a type of agreement.
